sections.paragraph:
Ἀνδρόνικος, ου, ὁ a common name (Diod S 19, 59, 2; Appian, Maced. 16; SIG2 and OGI indices; IPriene 313 [I b.c.]; Preisigke, Namenbuch; 2 Macc 4:31, 32, 34, 38; 5:23; Jos., Ant. 13, 75; 78) Andronicus, greeted in Ro 16:7; w. Junia or Junias described by Paul as συγγενεῖς μου καὶ συναιχμάλωτοι and called ἐπίσημοι ἐν τ. ἀποστόλοις.—BBacon, ET 42, ’31, 300–304. GBarton, ibid. 43, ’32, 359–61.—M-M.

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

IPriene IPriene = Die Inschriften von Priene—List 3

Preisigke, Namenbuch Preisigke, Namenbuch = Namenbuch 1922

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

ET ET = Expository Times—List 6

ibid. ibid. = ibidem (in the same book or passage)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 76
